rationale: The cited paper studies assortment size, option attractiveness, and cognitive decision costs, which is plausibly relevant to the citing paper’s focus on large choice sets and information/evaluation costs in detergent choice. However, the citing note’s stated prior-work linkage emphasizes rational-inattention theory papers (not this 2009 assortment experiment paper), and the only evidence here is a single DOI match with no in-text citation context. Given this sparse/mixed evidence, it is not safe to drop but not clearly a strong substantive link.
